commands.author: 100yenadmin

target.title: [Feature]: Channel Broker Phase 1 protocol foundation
target.body:
## Maintainer TL;DR This stack consolidates the recurring Telegram/Discord/Slack/WhatsApp/Signal/iMessage/etc. maintenance churn behind one Channel Broker contract. OpenClaw keeps the shared semantics that repeatedly regress across native channel plugins: sessions, allowlists, routing, /verbose, streaming policy, durable final sends, receipts, retries, and auditability. Broker providers own platform mechanics: bot APIs, bridge/device state, native ids, delivery quirks, and provider capabilities. This PR is the narrow foundation. It creates the versioned broker SDK subpath and bundled channel-broker plugin, but it does not move Telegram, Discord, Slack, or any user defaults. It also carries a test-only agent-support mock refresh for the current upstream CI shard that was already failing on raw upstream/main. The direction is intentionally incremental: first land the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, then prove conformance, then prove opt-in routing for the major channels, then model long-tail and constrained providers. Only after migration paths exist should legacy native-channel feature work freeze in #86115. ## Why This Is Better Today, one fix to streaming, /verbose, or delivery behavior can make Telegram fast this week and then break Discord, Slack, or another native plugin the next week. The broker path makes those shared semantics one OpenClaw-owned contract instead of many platform-specific re-implementations. When a provider adds support for previews, threads, receipts, media, or constrained account modes, that capability can become available through the same contract to routed channels whose provider declares and passes conformance for it. ## Stack Shape ```mermaid flowchart TD U["Umbrella issue #86105: reduce maintained-channel churn"] --> P1["#86096 Phase 1: SDK + bundled broker foundation"] P1 --> P2A["#86153 Phase 2A: conformance + signed inbound"] P2A --> P2B["#86154 Phase 2B: Telegram opt-in proof"] P2B --> P2C["#86156 Phase 2C: Discord opt-in proof"] P2C --> P2D["#86157 Phase 2D: Slack opt-in proof"] P2D --> P3["#86164 Phase 3: official/app capability matrix"] P3 --> P4["#86165 Phase 4: constrained provider capabilities"] P4 -. "future, not in this stack" .-> P5["Future freeze issue #86115: legacy native-plugin freeze"] ``` | PR | Role in the consolidation | | --- | --- | | #86096 | Creates the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, provider routing guard, media final-send mapping, SecretRef/cancellation handling, and prefix-ownership contract; carries a test-only upstream CI mock refresh. | | #86153 | Proves shared lifecycle conformance and adds signed inbound webhook registration/runtime handoff: inbound dedupe, durable final sends, receipts, retryable failures, preview/progress gates, and /verbose channelData. | | #86154 | Proves Telegram topics can route through broker targets without native Telegram internals. | | #86156 | Proves Discord DMs/channels/threads preserve conversation semantics through broker routing. | | #86157 | Proves Slack DMs/channel threads preserve direct/thread semantics through broker routing. | | #86164 | Adds official/app platform aliases, capability matrix rows, and declared delivery capability enforcement for long-tail channels. | | #86165 | Adds constrained-provider metadata for WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage, and bridge-backed providers. | ## Ownership Model ```mermaid flowchart LR Core["OpenClaw core
sessions, routing, allowlists
/verbose, streaming, retries
receipts, audit"] --> Broker["channel-broker plugin
openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-broker
V1 protocol"] Broker --> Provider["Provider-owned broker
HTTP/webhook transport
platform adapters"] Provider --> Platforms["Telegram, Discord, Slack
Teams, Matrix, WhatsApp
Signal, iMessage, long tail"] Provider --> Broker Broker --> Core ``` ## Review Shape These PRs are intentionally linear. GitHub still shows `main` as the base for each PR so maintainers can land them one by one from fork branches; the branches themselves are stacked, and maintainers can review the focused adjacent diffs here: | PR | Focused adjacent diff | | --- | --- | | #86153 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-protocol...feat/channel-broker-conformance | | #86154 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-conformance...feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing | | #86156 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing...feat/channel-broker-discord-routing | | #86157 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-discord-routing...feat/channel-broker-slack-routing | | #86164 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-slack-routing...feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities | | #86165 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities...feat/channel-broker-constrained-platform-capabilities | ## This PR - Phase: Phase 1 - SDK and bundled broker foundation - Role: Creates the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, provider routing guard, media final-send mapping, SecretRef/cancellation handling, and prefix-ownership contract; includes a test-only upstream CI mock refresh. - Head: `ee9d195bc0` ## Current Review State This PR is live for maintainer review. Current head: `ee9d195bc0`. Focused local validation for this phase is documented below. Broad validation remains owned by GitHub Actions. Native channel defaults remain unchanged unless this phase explicitly says otherwise. ## Summary Adds Phase 1 of the Channel Broker consolidation program: a versioned `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-broker` protocol surface, a bundled `channel-broker` channel plugin, provider-owned outbound request/receipt helpers, docs, and focused tests. This PR intentionally stays at the protocol/foundation layer. Telegram, Discord, Slack, and later channel migrations are tracked separately and should not be folded into this PR. ## Linked Context Closes #86107 Related #86105 Related #86108 Related #86109 Related #86110 Related #86111 Related #86112 Related #86113 Related #86114 Related #86115 ## Review Repair History The current Phase 1 branch includes focused repairs for ClawSweeper's blocker classes: - Preserves native prefix ownership when `channel-broker` declares platform aliases that overlap native plugins. If Telegram/Discord/Slack are registered, their prefixes stay native-owned; broker aliases own a platform prefix only when no native plugin declares it. - Adds the standard channel SecretRef contract for `channels.channel-broker.{outboundToken,signingSecret}` plus `accounts.*` and `providers.*` variants, and changes the broker schema/docs to use SecretRef-compatible credential input. - Threads `AbortSignal` through broker outbound requests, including the HTTP `fetch` transport and custom runtime override path. ## Real Behavior Proof - **Behavior or issue addressed:** Verified the Phase 1 broker surface resolves as a bundled channel and produces a normalized V1 outbound request for a Telegram-scoped broker target. This proves the user-visible foundation being shipped in this PR: catalog discovery, channel id normalization, target construction, protocol versioning, platform normalization, thread preservation, and final-send payload shape. - **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`, branch `feat/channel-broker-protocol`, after the current Phase 1 repair/doc series through `d39d47b3db`. - **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** ```bash node --import tsx -e 'const { listBundledChannelCatalogEntries } = await import("./src/channels/bundled-channel-catalog-read.ts"); const { normalizeChatChannelId } = await import("./src/channels/ids.ts"); const { buildBrokerConversationTarget, createBrokerOutboundRequest } = await import("./src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.ts"); const catalog = listBundledChannelCatalogEntries().find((entry) => entry.id === "channel-broker"); const target = buildBrokerConversationTarget({ platform: "Telegram", conversationId: "chat 123", threadId: "topic-7" }); const request = createBrokerOutboundRequest({ requestId: "send-proof-1", providerId: "synthetic-provider", platform: "Telegram", accountId: "default", conversation: { id: "chat 123", type: "channel", threadId: "topic-7" }, mode: "final", payloads: [{ text: "broker proof" }], requirements: { text: true, thread: true } }); console.log(JSON.stringify({ discoveredChannel: catalog && { id: catalog.id, order: catalog.order, docsPath: catalog.channel.docsPath }, channelNormalization: normalizeChatChannelId("channel-broker"), target, outboundProtocolVersion: request.version, outboundMode: request.mode, outboundPlatform: request.platform, outboundConversation: request.conversation, outboundPayloadText: request.payloads[0]?.text }, null, 2));' ``` - **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output copied from the command above: ```json { "discoveredChannel": { "id": "channel-broker", "order": 60, "docsPath": "/channels/channel-broker" }, "channelNormalization": "channel-broker", "target": "telegram:chat%20123?threadId=topic-7", "outboundProtocolVersion": 1, "outboundMode": "final", "outboundPlatform": "telegram", "outboundConversation": { "id": "chat 123", "type": "channel", "threadId": "topic-7" }, "outboundPayloadText": "broker proof" } ``` - **Observed result after fix:** OpenClaw discovered the bundled `channel-broker` entry, normalized the channel id, built a Telegram broker target that preserved `threadId`, and emitted a V1 final outbound request with normalized `platform: "telegram"`, `conversation.threadId: "topic-7"`, and payload text `broker proof`. - **What was not tested:** No real third-party broker provider delivery was tested in Phase 1. That is intentionally deferred to the Phase 2 migration proof issues for Telegram, Discord, and Slack. ## Focused Local Validation All local validation below ran from `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`: ```bash git diff --check ``` ```bash node node_modules/oxfmt/bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 --config .oxfmtrc.jsonc docs/channels/channel-broker.md docs/reference/secretref-credential-surface.md extensions/channel-broker/src/accounts.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/outbound.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/runtime.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/secret-contract.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/secret-contract.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/types.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-target-prefix.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-target-prefix.test.ts ``` ```bash pnpm exec vitest run extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/secret-contract.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts ``` ```bash pnpm exec vitest run src/infra/outbound/channel-target-prefix.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.config.ts ``` ```bash pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p extensions/channel-broker/tsconfig.json ``` ```bash pnpm exec vitest run test/scripts/bundled-plugin-build-entries.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.config.ts ``` ## Known Inherited Gates - `pnpm plugin-sdk:surface:check` has an inherited clean-upstream SDK budget drift; this PR adds the intended `plugin-sdk/channel-broker` public subpath. - `pnpm docs:check-i18n-glossary` has inherited unrelated glossary-label failures on clean upstream; Channel Broker glossary entries were added in this branch.
target.author: 100yenadmin
